Name: Blaziken
Effects: Search your discard pile for a [R] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon.
Discard 2 [R] Energy attached to Blaziken. This attack does 80 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Flames spout from its wrists, enveloping its knuckles. Its punches scorch its foes.

Name: Cresselia
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, remove 2 damage counters from Cresselia.
You may discard 2 Energy attached to Cresselia. If you do, remove all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Shiny particles are released from its wings like a veil. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

Name: Darkrai
Effects: Search your deck for an Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
If the Defending Pokémon is Cresselia, this attack’s base damage is 100.
Flavor-Text: It can lull people to sleep and make them dream. It is active during nights of the new moon.

Name: Darkrai
Effects: You may have your opponent switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 or his or her Benched Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon is Asleep, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage. If the Defending Pokémon is not Asleep, that Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It can lull people to sleep and make them dream. It is active during nights of the new moon.

Name: Pachirisu
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

If the Defending Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage. Discard that Pokémon Tool card, look at your opponent’s hand, and discard any Pokémon Tool cards of the same name you find there.
Flavor-Text: It makes fur balls that crackle with static electricity. It stores them with berries in tree holes.

Name: Porygon-Z
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a basic Energy card from your hand. Porygon-Z is the same type as that Energy Card until the end of your turn. This power can’t be used if Porygon-Z is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Additional software was installed to make it a better Pokémon. It began acting oddly, however.

Name: Rotom
Effects: Choose 1 card from your opponent’s hand without looking. Look at that card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

Name: Sceptile
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Each basic [G] Energy card attached to your [G] Pokémon provides [G][G] Energy instead. You can’t use more than 1 Wild Growth Poké-Body each turn.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The leaves that grow on its arms can slice down thick trees. It is without peer in jungle combat.

Name: Swampert
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a [W] or [F] Energy attached to 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Swampert is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Discard the top card from your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: It can swim while towing a large ship. It bashes down foes with a swing of its thick arms.

Name: Tangrowth
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage for each basic Energy card attached to Tangrowth to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Remove 3 damage counters from Tangrowth. The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It ensnares prey by extending arms made of vines. Losing arms to predators does not trouble it.

Name: Togekiss
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Togekiss from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may look at the top 10 cards of your deck, choose as many basic Energy cards as you like, and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Put the other cards back on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage. If tails, remove 3 damage counters from Togekiss.
Flavor-Text: It will never appear where there is strife. Its sightings have become rare recently.

Name: Altaria
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Altaria by attacks from your opponent’s Evolved Pokémon is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It looks like a fluffy cloud when it is in flight. It hums with its soprano voice.

Name: Beedrill
Effects: Does 30 damage times the number of Beedrill you have in play.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Its best attack involves flying around at high speed, striking with poison needles, then flying off.

Name: Butterfree
Effects: After your attack, remove from 1 of your Pokémon the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It loves the honey of flowers and can locate flower patches that have even tiny amounts of pollen.

Name: Claydol
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose up to 2 cards from your hand and put them on the bottom of your deck in any order. If you do, draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. This power can’t be used if Claydol is affected by a Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: An ancient clay figurine that came to life as a Pokémon from exposure to a mysterious ray of light.

Name: Dialga
Effects: Draw a card.
You may return all Energy cards attached to Dialga to your hand. If you do, remove the highest Stage Evolution card from the Defending Pokémon and shuffle that card into your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: It has the power to control time. It appears in Sinnoh-region myths as an ancient deity.

Name: Exploud
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If the first coin is heads, this attack does 50 damage to the Defending Pokémon. If the first coin is tails, this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) If the second coin is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If the second coin is tails, your opponent can’t play any Trainer, Supporter, or Stadium cards from his or her hand during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Its howls can be heard over six miles away. It emits all sorts of noises from the ports on its body.

Name: Houndoom
Effects: Choose 1 card from your opponent’s hand without looking and discard it.

You may discard a [R] or [D] Energy attached to Houndoom. If you discard a [R] Energy, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned. If you discard a [D] Energy, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Long ago, people imagined its eerie howls to be the call of the grim reaper.

Name: Hypno
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is Asleep, move all damage counters from Hypno to the Defending Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Seeing its swinging pendulum can induce sleep in three seconds, even in someone who just woke up.

Name: Kingler
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon would be be Knocked Out by this attack, Kingler does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The larger pincer has 10,000-horsepower strength. However, it is so heavy, it is difficult to aim.

Name: Lapras
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon Tool Card, a Supporter card, and a basic Energy card, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It loves crossing the sea with people and Pokémon on its back. It understands human speech.

Name: Latias
Effects: Remove 2 damage counters and all Special Conditions from Latias.
Discard a [R] Energy and a [W] Energy attached to Latias.
Flavor-Text: Its body is covered with a down that can refract light in such a way that it becomes invisible.

Name: Latios
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to Latios. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Discard 3 Energy attached to Latios.
Flavor-Text: A highly intelligent Pokémon. By folding back its wings in flight, it can overtake jet planes.

Name: Mawile
Effects: Choose 1 face-down Prize card (yours or your opponent’s) and put it face up. If that card is a Supporter card, use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack. (That card remains face up for the rest of the game.)
During your next turn, if an attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), that attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Attached to its head is a huge set of jaws formed by horns. It can chew through iron beams.

Name: Milotic
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Milotic by your opponent’s Pokémon LV.X.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both are tails, this attack does nothing. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When people bicker, it is said to arise from the depths of lakes to becalm violent hearts.

Name: Palkia
Effects: Search your deck for a Stadium card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. If there is any Stadium card in play, discard it.

You may flip a coin. If heads, discard all Energy attached to Palkia and put the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it on top of your opponent’s deck. Your opponent shuffles his or her deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It has the ability to distort space. It is described as a deity in Sinnoh-region mythology.

Name: Primeape
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Primeape has any damage counters on it, Primeape’s attacks do 40 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Primeape is now Confused. Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It grows angry if you see its eyes and gets angrier if you run. If you beat it, it gets even madder.

Name: Slowking
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent’s last turn, search your deck for any 1 card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can’t be used if Slowking is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: During your next turn, Slowking’s Psych Up attack’s base damage is 60.
Flavor-Text: Being bitten by Shellder gave it intelligence comparable to that of award-winning scientists.

Name: Unown H
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Unown H, Unown E, Unown A, and Unown L on your Bench, you may remove all Special Conditions from 1 of your Active Pokémon.
Effects: Discard a card from your hand. (If you can’t discard a card from your hand, this attack does nothing.)
Flavor-Text: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

Name: Wailord
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Wailord remains Asleep between turns, remove 1 damage counter from Wailord.
Effects: If Wailord has any damage counters on it, Wailord is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: The biggest of all Pokémon. It can dive to a depth of almost 10,000 feet on only one breath.

Name: Weezing
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: At any time between turns, each player puts 1 more damage counter on his or her Poisoned Pokémon.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It grows by feeding on gases released by garbage. Though very rare, triplets have been found.

Name: Wigglytuff
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this power. Each Active Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) is now Asleep. This power can’t be used if Wigglytuff is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: If Igglybuff is anywhere under Wigglytuff, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its fine fur feels sublime to the touch. It can expand its body by inhaling air.

Name: Arbok
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 10 damage plus 40 more damage. If the Defending Pokémon is not Poisoned, that Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The pattern on its belly is for intimidation. It constricts foes while they are frozen in fear.

Name: Cacturne
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It becomes active at night, seeking prey that is exhausted from the day’s desert heat.

Name: Combusken
Effects: During your next turn, each of Combusken’s attacks does 30 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Its kicking mastery lets it loose 10 kicks per second. It emits sharp cries to intimidate foes.

Name: Delibird
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for any 1 card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It carries food rolled up in its tail. It has the habit of sharing food with people lost in mountains.

Name: Floatzel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Floatzel during your opponent’s next turn.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It floats using its well-developed floatation sac. It assists in the rescues of drowning people.

Name: Gorebyss
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to the new Defending Pokémon.
If Gorebyss has any [P] Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It lives at the bottom of the sea. In the springtime, its pink body turns more vivid for some reason.

Name: Granbull
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Granbull.
Flavor-Text: It is timid in spite of its looks. If it becomes enraged, however, it will strike with its huge fangs.

Name: Grovyle
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [G] Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It lives in dense jungles. While closing in on its prey, it leaps from branch to branch.

Name: Hariyama
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 40 more damage.
If Hariyama has fewer remaining HP than the Defending Pokémon, this attack’s base damage is 80.
Flavor-Text: It loves to match power with big-bodied Pokémon. It can knock a truck flying with its arm thrusts.

Name: Huntail
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack or retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
If Huntail has any [D] Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage and discard a Special Energy card, if any, attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lives deep in the sea. With a tail shaped like a small fish, it attracts unsuspecting prey.

Name: Linoone
Effects: Move a Pokémon Tool card attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon to another of your opponent’s Pokémon (excluding Pokémon that already has a Pokémon Tool attached to it). (If an effect of this attack is prevented, this attack does nothing.)
Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It charges prey at speeds over 60 mph. However, because it can only run straight, it often fails.

Name: Loudred
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The shock waves from its cries can tip over trucks. It stamps its feet to power up.

Name: Magcargo
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Magcargo can’t be Asleep or Paralyzed.
Effects: You may discard the top card of your deck. If you do, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Its body temperature is roughly 18,000 degrees F. Flames spout from gaps in its hardened shell.

Name: Marshtomp
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon and 10 damage to each of your opponent’s other Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its sturdy legs give it sure footing, even in mud. It burrows into dirt to sleep.

Name: Metapod
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Metapod is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card that evolves from Metapod and put it onto Metapod. (This counts as evolving Metapod.) Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can’t be used if Metapod is affected by a Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: A steel-hard shell protects its tender body. It quietly endures hardships while awaiting evolution.

Name: Pelipper
Effects: Choose up to 2 basic [W] Energy cards from your hand and attach them to Pelipper. Remove 2 damage counters for each Energy card attached in this way.
Does 30 times the number of Energy cards attached to Pelipper. Then, put all Energy cards attached to Pelipper on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It dips its large bill in the sea, then scoops up numerous prey along with water.

Name: Porygon2
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a Supporter card from your hand and use the effect of that card as the effect of this power. This power can’t be used if Porygon2 is affected by a Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: With planetary development software installed, it became capable of working in space.

Name: Purugly
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It is a brazen brute that barges its way into another Pokémon’s nest and claims it as its own.

Name: Relicanth
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Trainer Cards that have Fossil in their names, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Both Relicanth and the Defending Pokémon are now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: A rare Pokémon discovered during a deep-sea exploration. It has not changed in over 100 million years.

Name: Seviper
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can’t play any Trainer cards from his or her hand during your opponent’s next turn.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: For many generations, it has feuded with ZANGOOSE. It whets its bladed tail on rocks for battle.

Name: Skarmory
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Skarmory by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Despite being clad entirely in iron-hard armor, it flies at speeds over 180 mph.

Name: Slowbro
Effects: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks. That Pokémon can’t use that attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Though usually dim witted, it seems to become inspired if the SHELLDER on its tail bites down.

Name: Togetic
Effects: Your opponent may draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It is said to appear in front of kindly people to scatter a glowing down called “joy dust.”

Name: Unown F
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown F is on your Bench, you may use this power. Put a coin next to your Active Pokémon without showing your opponent and cover it with your hand. Your opponent guesses if the coin is heads or tails. If he or she is wrong, draw a card.
Effects: You may flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, this attack’s base damage is 30.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

Name: Unown G
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown G is on your Bench, you may discard all cards attached to Unown G and attach Unown G to 1 of your Pokémon as a Pokémon Tool card. As long as Unown G is attached to a Pokémon, prevent all effects of attacks, excluding damage, done to that Pokémon.
Effects: If Unown G has any damage counters on it, this attack’s base damage is 10.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

Name: Wailmer
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: On sunny days, it lands on beaches to bounce like a ball and play. It spouts water from its nose.

Name: Zangoose
Effects: If your opponent has Seviper in play, this attack does 40 damage plus 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has feuded with SEVIPER for many generations. Its sharp claws are its biggest weapons.

Name: Baltoy
Effects: If you have less cards in your hand than your opponent, draw cards until you have the same number of cards as your opponent. (If you have more or the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does nothing.)
Switch Baltoy with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It moves by spinning on its foot. It is a rare Pokémon that was discovered in ancient ruins.

Name: Buizel
Flavor-Text: It has a flotation sac that is like an inflatable collar. It floats on water with its head out.

Name: Cacnea
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: By storing water in its body, this desert dweller can survive 30 days without water.

Name: Caterpie
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Caterpie is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card that evolves from Caterpie and put it onto Caterpie. (This counts as evolving Caterpie.) Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can’t be used if Caterpie is affected by a Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: It releases a stench from its red antenna to repel enemies. It grows by molting repeatedly.

Name: Clamperl
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It makes a single pearl during its lifetime. The pearl is said to amplify psychic power.

Name: Drowzee
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It can tell what people are dreaming by sniffing with its big nose. It loves fun dreams.

Name: Ekans
Effects: Remove 2 damage counters from Ekans.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It sneaks through grass without making a sound and strikes unsuspecting prey from behind.

Name: Feebas
Effects: Search your deck for an Evolution card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It is famous for its shabby appearance. While populous, they tend to cluster in set locations.

Name: Glameow
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It claws if displeased and purrs when affectionate. Its fickleness is very popular among some.

Name: Houndour
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a [R] Energy attached to Houndour.
Flavor-Text: It conveys its feelings using different cries. It works in a pack to cleverly take down prey.

Name: Igglybuff
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Jigglypuff from your hand onto Igglybuff (this counts as evolving Igglybuff) and remove all damage counters from Igglybuff.
Effects: Draw a card. If you didn’t play any Supporter card from your hand during this turn, draw 2 more cards.
Flavor-Text: It has a soft and bouncy body. Once it starts bouncing, it becomes impossible to stop.

Name: Illumise
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a [G] Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can’t be used if Illumise is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon has any Poké-Bodies, that Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: With its sweet aroma, it guides VOLBEAT to draw signs with light in the night sky.

Name: Jigglypuff
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Jigglypuff by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: When it wavers its big, round eyes, it begins singing a lullaby that makes everyone drowsy.

Name: Kakuna
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Kakuna by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: While awaiting evolution, it hides from predators under leaves and in nooks of branches.

Name: Koffing
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Lighter-than-air gases in its body keep it aloft. The gases not only smell, they are also explosive.

Name: Krabby
Flavor-Text: It lives in burrows dug on sandy beaches. Its pincers fully grow back if they are broken in battle.

Name: Lunatone
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a card from your hand. Then, if you have Solrock in play, draw a card. This power can’t be used if Lunatone is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: You may discard any Stadium card in play.
Flavor-Text: Because it turns active on nights of the full moon, it is said to have some link to the lunar phases.

Name: Luvdisc
Effects: Reveal the top card of your deck and put it into your hand. If that card is a Pokémon, draw 2 cards.
If Luvdisc and the Defending Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached to them, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lives in warm seas. It is said that a couple finding this Pokémon will be blessed with eternal love.

Name: Makuhita
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It toughens its body by slamming into thick trees. Many snapped trees can be found near its nest.

Name: Mankey
Effects: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Mankey.
Flavor-Text: It lives in treetop colonies. If one becomes enraged, the whole colony rampages for no reason.

Name: Mudkip
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: To alert it, the fin on its head senses the flow of water. It has the strength to heft boulders.

Name: Porygon
Effects: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.
Flavor-Text: The world’s first artificially created Pokémon. It can travel through electronic space.

Name: Slowpoke
Effects: Remove all Special Conditions and 3 damage counters from Slowpoke. Slowpoke is now Asleep.
Slowpoke can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: Although slow, it is skilled at fishing with its tail. It does not feel pain if its tail is bitten.

Name: Slugma
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Its body is made of magma. If it doesn’t keep moving, its body will cool and harden.

Name: Snubbull
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Small Pokémon flee from its scary face. It is, however, considered by women to be cute.

Name: Solrock
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If you have Lunatone in play, damage done to your opponent’s Pokémon by your [P] or [F] Pokémon isn’t affected by resistance.
Effects: Search your discard pile for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A new Pokémon species, rumored to be from the sun. It gives off light while spinning.

Name: Swablu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Swablu during your opponent’s next turn.
Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its wings are like cotton tufts. If it perches on someone’s head, it looks like a cotton hat.

Name: Tangela
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon.
Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each [C] Energy in the Defending Pokémon’s Retreat Cost (after applying effects to the Retreat Cost).
Flavor-Text: It is shrouded by blue vines. No one has seen the face hidden behind this growth of vines.

Name: Togepi
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Its shell is said to be stuffed with happiness that it shares with kindhearted people.

Name: Torchic
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees F.

Name: Treecko
Effects: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to Treecko but not used to pay for this attack’s Energy cost. You can’t add more than 20 damage in this way.
Remove 1 damage counter from Treecko.
Flavor-Text: The soles of its feet are covered by countless tiny spikes, enabling it to walk on walls and ceilings.

Name: Unown L
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Unown L can use any attack from any Unown in play (both yours and your opponent’s). (You still have to pay for that attack’s Energy cost.)
Effects: Search either player’s discard pile for up to any 2 cards, show them to your opponent, and put them on top of that player’s deck in any order you like.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

Name: Volbeat
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Illumise in play, you may search your discard pile for a Supporter card, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck. This power can’t be used if Volbeat is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It communicates with others by lighting up its rear at night. It loves ILLUMISE’s sweet aroma.

Name: Weedle
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It eats its weight in leaves every day. It fends off attackers with the needle on its head.

Name: Whismur
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Usually, its cries are like quiet murmurs. If frightened, it shrieks at the same volume as a jet plane.

Name: Wingull
Flavor-Text: It makes its nest on steep sea cliffs. Riding updrafts, it soars to great heights.

Name: Zigzagoon
Effects: Search your discard pile for any 1 card, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It walks in zigzag fashion. It is good at finding items in the grass and even in the ground.

Name: Amulet Coin
Description: If the Pokémon Amulet Coin is attached to is your Active Pokémon at the end of your turn, draw a card.
Tool-Rule: Attach Amulet Coin to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

Name: Felicity’s Drawing
Description: Discard up to 2 cards from your hand. If you discard 1 card, draw 3 cards. If you discard 2 cards, draw 4 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Leftovers
Description: If the Pokémon Leftovers is attached to is your Active Pokémon at the end of your turn, remove 1 damage counter from the Pokémon.
Tool-Rule: Attach Leftovers to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

Name: Moonlight Stadium
Description: The Retreat Cost for each [P] and [D] Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) is 0.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Premier Ball
Description: Search your deck or your discard pile for a Pokémon LV.X, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. If you search your deck, shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Rare Candy
Description: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 1 or Stage 2 card that evolves from that Pokémon in your hand, put that card on the Basic Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.)

Name: Cresselia LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from either player’s Pokémon to another Pokémon (yours or your opponent’s). This power can’t be used if Cresselia is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out by this attack, take 1 more Prize card.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Cresselia. Cresselia LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Darkrai LV.X
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Each basic [D] Energy card attached to your [D] Pokémon now has the effect “If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).” You can’t use more than 1 Dark Shadow Poké-Body each turn.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. Flip 2 coins instead of 1 between turns. If either of them is tails, the Defending Pokémon is still Asleep. If both of them are tails, the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Darkrai. Darkrai LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Dialga LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may have your opponent flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your turn ends. If both of them are tails, after your opponent draws a card at the beginning of his or her next turn, his or her turn ends. This power can’t be used if Dialga is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: During your next turn, Dialga can’t use Metal Flash.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Dialga. Dialga LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Palkia LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may have your opponent switch 1 of your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, you switch 1 of the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Palkia is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: You may move all Energy cards attached to Palkia to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. (Ignore this effect if you don’t have any Benched Pokémon.)
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Palkia. Palkia LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.
